_meta.policy: Use only entries where quote.verified is true. Keep author visible when present. Keep source and context available for audit.
notes: Quotes are intentionally short and source-traceable.
article_fit indicates how naturally the quote fits the article theme, not whether the quote itself is authentic.
source_quality distinguishes official, scholarly, and reputable secondary sources.

quote.text: Nature is a big book from which we can draw inspiration.
quote.author: Hector Guimard
quote.context: Very strong fit for Guimard and for a facade read through organic Art Nouveau principles.

quote.text: the learned game, correct and magnificent, of forms assembled in the light
quote.author: Le Corbusier
quote.attribution: Vers une architecture / formulation reused by the Fondation Le Corbusier
quote.context: Good thematic fit for a facade article centered on composition, massing, and street presence, even though the quote is not specific to Art Deco.

quote.text: a house must reflect its dweller's life and be his portrait
quote.author: Victor Horta
quote.attribution: On the Winssinger House / memoir formulation
quote.context: Strong fit for a Brussels domestic Art Nouveau article, especially where the facade is read as a precise urban presence.

quote.author: Henry van de Velde
quote.context: Very strong fit for a facade article structured by line, ascent, and controlled ornamental tension.

quote.text: Have nothing in your houses that you do not know to be useful or believe to be beautiful.
quote.author: William Morris
quote.context: Good fit for a domestic facade where floral decoration is treated as part of the identity of the house rather than as an added flourish.

quote.text: Have nothing in your houses that you do not know to be useful or believe to be beautiful.
quote.author: William Morris
quote.context: Good fit for an article about floral motif, domestic facade, and decorative order kept under control.

quote.text: I have loved my Art, my Intellectual Children more than Myself.
quote.author: Victor Horta
quote.context: Useful as a short, high-register Belgian Art Nouveau citation; fit is thematic rather than architect-specific to Strauven.

quote.text: Der Zeit ihre Kunst. Der Kunst ihre Freiheit.
quote.author: Vienna Secession motto
quote.attribution: Facade inscription / To each era its art. To art its freedom.
quote.context: Ideal fit: this is the building's own inscription and one of the strongest possible editorial anchors for the article.

quote.text: Only that which is practical can be beautiful.
quote.author: Otto Wagner
quote.context: Very strong fit for Otto Wagner and especially apt for the Majolikahaus, where decorative surface remains tied to architectural clarity.

quote.text: Have nothing in your houses that you do not know to be useful or believe to be beautiful.
quote.author: William Morris
quote.context: Good fit for Liberty architecture in Milan, especially where ornament and domestic dignity are both central to the reading.

quote.text: Is not a noble and simple dignity sufficient?
quote.author: Louis Sullivan
quote.context: Strong fit for a facade article built on restraint, lettering, and composition rather than decorative overflow.

quote.text: Only that which is practical can be beautiful.
quote.author: Otto Wagner
quote.context: Good fit for a public building article where civic function and architectural expression are read together.

quote.text: a house must reflect its dweller's life and be his portrait
quote.author: Victor Horta
quote.attribution: On the Winssinger House / memoir formulation
quote.context: Good fit for an article where commanditaire, use, and facade reading are tightly linked.

quote.text: the learned game, correct and magnificent, of forms assembled in the light
quote.author: Le Corbusier
quote.attribution: Vers une architecture / formulation reused by the Fondation Le Corbusier
quote.context: Useful as a general architecture quote for an Art Deco facade article focused on composition, framing, and visual presence. Building identification itself remains unresolved.
